title: Gymnanthemum secundifolium
intro: Gymnanthemum secundifolium, also known as African blue basil, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, African blue basil has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, African blue basil is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried powder, and is also used in aromatherapy and as a culinary herb.
taxonomy: Gymnanthemum secundifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnanthemum, which is a group of plants within the tribe Eupatorieae. The plant is native to parts of Asia and is also found in other regions. The classification of Gymnanthemum secundifolium is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant classification system helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.
uses: Gymnanthemum secundifolium is native to Africa and Asia, but its medicinal uses are not well-documented.
